assumptions: The intragroup dividend has been declared and has resulted in recognition of an intragroup receivable/payable.
The question is about designation of foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
The group is assumed not to be an investment entity unless stated otherwise.
Any hedge relationship would also need to satisfy the general IFRS 9 designation, documentation and effectiveness requirements.

approaches.label: General intragroup hedged item prohibition
approaches.reasoning: In consolidated financial statements, hedged items generally must involve parties external to the reporting entity. Intragroup items are excluded unless a specific exception applies.
approaches.practical_implication: An intragroup dividend receivable cannot be designated in consolidation under the general rule alone.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.5 restricts hedged items in consolidated financial statements to exposures with external parties, subject to stated exceptions.

approaches.label: Intragroup monetary item foreign currency hedge
approaches.reasoning: IFRS 9 specifically allows the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item in consolidated financial statements when the resulting FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
conditions: the declared dividend creates a monetary receivable/payable
the entities involved have different functional currencies
the FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation
only the foreign currency risk is designated
the item is reliably measurable and the hedge is formally documented and effective
approaches.practical_implication: A recorded intragroup dividend receivable may be eligible only through the intragroup monetary item FX exception.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.5 provides the baseline exclusion of intragroup items in consolidated financial statements.
Paragraph 6.3.6 permits the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item if the related FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
Paragraphs 6.3.2 and 6.4.1 require reliable measurement, formal designation, documentation and hedge effectiveness.

approaches.label: Forecast intragroup transaction cash flow hedge
approaches.reasoning: The forecast intragroup transaction exception is limited to highly probable transactions whose FX risk affects consolidated profit or loss. A recognized dividend receivable is no longer a forecast transaction.
approaches.practical_implication: A recorded intragroup dividend receivable should not be analyzed as a cash flow hedge of a forecast intragroup transaction.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.3.6 and B6.3.5 allow a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction only when it is in another currency and its FX risk affects consolidated profit or loss.
Paragraph 6.5.2 distinguishes cash flow hedges of forecast transactions from hedges of recognized items.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.reasoning: A net investment hedge concerns foreign currency risk on qualifying net assets of a foreign operation, not an intragroup dividend receivable arising between group entities.
approaches.practical_implication: The dividend receivable should not be documented as a net investment hedge unless the actual hedged item is the qualifying net investment itself.

source_contributions.summary: Paragraph 6.5.2(c) identifies hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge type.
Paragraphs 10-14 limit the hedged item to an amount of net assets of a foreign operation and the hedged risk to foreign currency differences between functional currencies.
